meta.title: Support — GitKit
meta.description: Support and troubleshooting for GitFolder and GitKanban. Most issues come down to git, token access, repository URLs, or folder permissions.

title: Support
intro: GitFolder and GitKanban are small, local-first apps. Most issues come down to git, token access, repository URLs, or folder permissions. Pick your app below.

steps.title: Verify Git is installed
steps.descHtml: Open Terminal and run git --version. If not found, install Xcode Command Line Tools: xcode-select --install.

steps.title: Test GitHub access
steps.descHtml: Open GitFolder Settings, paste a fine-grained GitHub token, enter the repository URL, and run Test GitHub Access.

steps.title: Check the repository URL
steps.descHtml: Make sure the URL in GitFolder matches the HTTPS URL on your GitHub repository page. It should start with https://github.com/. SSH URLs are available under Advanced settings.

items.title: Folder shows "Error" status
items.descHtml: Open Settings and check the error message. The most common causes are a missing token, a token without repository access, an incorrect repository URL, or a folder permission that needs to be refreshed.

items.title: Folder shows "Needs attention"
items.descHtml: There's a Git conflict. Resolve it in your preferred Git client, then resume syncing in GitFolder.

items.title: Changes not appearing on GitHub
items.descHtml: Check that the folder isn't paused, the sync interval hasn't elapsed yet, and the repository URL is correct. You can force a sync with "Sync Now".

gitkanban.heading: GitKanban
gitkanban.title: In development
gitkanban.bodyHtml: GitKanban's native macOS app is still being built, so there's nothing to install yet. The board format and core logic are open on GitHub — that's the best place to follow progress, report issues, or ask questions. Want a heads-up when it ships? Notify me.

stillStuck.title: Still stuck?
stillStuck.bodyHtml: Email me@sil.mt or open an issue on GitHub with the error message from Settings and your macOS version. Technical git output is kept in local logs so sync errors can be diagnosed without exposing details in the menu bar.
